title: New Mexico Kicks Off Public Hearing for Proposed CTFS Regulations
content: The New Mexico Environmental Improvement Board on Sept. 22 kicked off a public hearing to gather public input on a proposed rule to implement the stateâ€™s Clean Transportation Fuel Program. New Mexico became the fourth U.S. state to establish a clean fuel standard (CFS) in March 2024 when Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham signed a bill creating a technology-neutral clean transportation fuel standard (CTFS). The program aims to reduce the carbon intensity (CI) of transportation fuels by at least 20% by 2030 and at least 30% by 2040, when compared to a 2018 baseline. The New Mexico Environment Department in December 2024 released a discussion draft rule, with a public comment period that was open through Jan. 17. A proposed rule was released in May 2025. In June, the EIB announced plans to hold a public hearing beginning Sept. 22 to gather public input on the proposal. The hearing is scheduled to continue through Oct. 3 and then resume on Nov. 17 and continue as long as required to hear all testimony, evidence and public comment. The hearing is being conducted in a hybrid format to allow for both in person and virtual participation.
author: Analysts Account
